title: Security at airport takes precedence over devotional right, says Bombay HC
description: The petition was moved by the Taxi-Rickshaw Ola-Uber Men’s Union, which contended that a temporary shed in the airport vicinity, historically used by drivers for congregational prayers, was demolished by civic authorities last year.

title: ‘Mission to sink navy’: US Central Command shares video of strike on Iranian drone carrier- watch
description: United States forces have struck an Iranian drone carrier, causing it to catch fire. This action is part of intensified military operations against the Iranian Navy. Over thirty Iranian warships have been sunk or destroyed. Attacks from Iran have significantly decreased. United States firepower over Iran is expected to surge dramatically as the conflict continues.

Why Iran's global partners are staying on the sidelines
description: Iran's long-standing allies, including Turkiye, India, China, and Russia, are offering little more than words as the Islamic Republic faces attacks. These relationships, largely transactional and driven by necessity, lack the deep commitments needed for mutual defense. Experts suggest Iran's foreign policy, focused on militias rather than state alliances, has left it isolated when facing direct threats.

description: Dolphin Nature Conservation Society (DNCS), which maintains the park in collaboration with VMRDA and RCD Government Hospital, organised the workshop.

description: The vote was 219 to 212, largely along party lines, in the House, where Mr. Trump’s fellow Republicans ‌control a narrow majority of seats; two Republicans voted in favor of the resolution and four Democrats voted against it

title: Iran missile fire sparks explosions over Tel Aviv, no reported injuries
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on March 5, that his country was prepared for any potential ground invasion, saying such a move would spell “disaster” for the U.S. and Israel

title: Trump demands say on Iran's next supreme leader as Israel-Iran war ripples across West Asia

title: Sinking of Iranian ship: The legal framework that governs maritime conflict
‘No one will be able to forcibly convert Hindu girls’: Maharashtra Cabinet clears ‘anti-love jihad’ Bill
Jacob Bethell's blazing century rewrites record books in T20 World Cup semi final
description: Jacob Bethell's electrifying 105 off 48 balls, the fastest T20 World Cup century by an England batter, nearly secured a historic win against India. Despite his record-breaking knock, England fell short by seven runs in a thrilling semi-final chase of 254. India's strong batting and disciplined bowling ultimately prevailed in a high-scoring encounter.

title: Bulldozers on Bhoodan lands kick up political dust
description: Nearly 600 houses were razed in a massive demolition drive at a village near Khammam city on February 24, rendering over 1,800 people homeless. While officials say the action was taken to clear unauthorised structures and reclaim land worth over ₹250 crore, the displaced families, many of whom claim to hold pattas issued by the erstwhile Andhra Pradesh Bhoodan Board in 2014, allege they were not given any notice. The demolitions have sparked protests, sharp political reactions and renewed debate over the fate of lands once donated for the landless under the historic Bhoodan movement, writes P. Sridhar

title: U.S. issues 30-day waiver to allow India to purchase Russian oil amid West Asian supply woes
description: Scott Bessent said the move aims to enable oil to keep flowing into the global market amid the war in West Asia that has disrupted oil supply

title: US gives India 30-day waiver to purchase Russian oil amid Middle East crisis
description: The US has granted India a 30-day waiver to continue buying Russian oil, aiming to stabilize global energy markets amid Middle East tensions. This move allows Indian refiners to secure crucial supplies, with Washington anticipating increased future energy imports from the US. The waiver addresses potential disruptions to vital shipping routes like the Strait of Hormuz.

title: 40 years ago March 06, 1986: Rajneesh arrested in Greece

description: Exporters ask Centre to recognise current logistics disruption as a force majeure type; close to 90% of total rice exports to West Asia, worth about ₹25,000 crore annually, is basmati rice

title: Nepal election: Voting concludes with preliminary turnout around 60%
description: The Election Commission had set up 23,112 polling centres at 10,963 polling stations across 77 districts

title: Why north Karnataka’s under-nutrition burden stays high
description: In north Karnataka districts, child undernutrition has been an unrelenting problem for decades. More than a function of food scarcity, it is an outcome of overlapping issues, some inter-generational and some structural, writes Shilpa Elizabeth

title: A trailblazer for the rights of  Dalits and the empowerment of women
description: Meenambal Sivaraj was born on December 12, 1904, in Rangoon, in a family that placed great importance on education and service. One of her most significant contributions was her work with All India Scheduled Castes Federation founded by  Ambedkar

title: Days ahead of polls, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal get new Governors

Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda share reception video
description: On March 4th, the newlyweds Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda marked their love story with an extravagant reception in Hyderabad. Accompanied by a heartfelt video montage, they expressed their heartfelt gratitude for all the love and support from fans and friends alike.

Denied medical leave, UP loco pilot pulls down pants to show ‘proof’ of piles surgery
description: A loco pilot, recovering from surgery, faced a senior official's demand for proof of illness for sick leave extension. In a desperate act, he revealed his surgical wound. The incident, caught on video, went viral, leading to his brief suspension, which was later revoked after union intervention. He claims repeated demands for proof.

title: 'Heart rate would've been 160-175': Suryakumar Yadav opens up after India win
description: India secured a thrilling seven-run victory over England in the T20 World Cup semi-final, defending a massive 253/7. Despite Jacob Bethell's explosive 105, England fell just short at 246/7. Sanju Samson's 89 laid the foundation for India's formidable total, setting up a final clash against New Zealand.

Jasprit Bumrah hits golden milestone, scripts history in India vs England semi-final
description: Jasprit Bumrah achieved a historic milestone, claiming his 500th international wicket during the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final against England. This feat places him among India's elite bowlers, reinforcing his status as a formidable modern-era fast bowler. Bumrah's consistent performance across formats continues to be a significant asset for the Indian team.

title: 'India don’t give it to ICC': Ex-Pak batter targets BCCI over doping tests
description: Pakistan cricketer Ahmed Shehzad sparked controversy by claiming the Board of Control for Cricket in India does not allow the International Cricket Council to conduct doping tests on Indian players. However, India’s testing system operates under the National Anti-Doping Agency, which follows global anti-doping rules set by the World Anti-Doping Agency.

title: 'Jailer' star Shiva Rajkumar says, 'Actors not objects'
description: Kantara actress Sapthami Gowda has voiced concerns over inappropriate filming angles by paparazzi at public events, sparking a wider conversation. Veteran actor Shiva Rajkumar publicly supported her, urging media to respect women and avoid objectification. Other actresses have also joined the discussion, highlighting the need for dignity for female artists.

title: Delhi records season’s hottest day at 34.3°c, 7°c above normal; night temperatures up too
description: Delhi experienced its hottest day of the season with the maximum temperature reaching 34.3°C, seven degrees above normal. This sharp rise is attributed to clear skies and dry weather, with temperatures expected to climb further. Night temperatures are also trending upwards, settling at 18.2°C, five degrees above normal.

description: In an approach that reduces food waste and improves food security, researchers have turned discarded carrot parts into vegan patties made from pink oyster mushroom mycelia, offering a protein-rich alternative

Holi horror: Delhi man beaten to death after balloon water splashes woman
description: In a tragic turn of events during the vibrant Holi festivities in Uttam Nagar, Delhi, a simple splash from a water balloon ignited a deadly clash between two communities. The confrontation resulted in the brutal beating of 26-year-old Tarun, culminating in his tragic death.

title: Sanju Samson scripts history, takes India to T20 World Cup final
description: Sanju Samson smashed a brilliant 89 off 42 balls in the T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final against England. His innings equalled Virat Kohli's record for the highest score by an Indian in a knockout match. Samson also set a new record for most sixes by an Indian in a single T20 World Cup tournament.

title: Bihar: JDU cadre bet on Nishant to fill in father Nitish Kumar's shoes
description: Bihar's ruling party, JDU, anticipates Nishant Kumar's political debut as his father, Nitish Kumar, prepares to step down. This move aims to reassure party workers and maintain the JDU's traditional support base, particularly among the Kurmi community. Nishant, an engineering graduate, is reportedly being considered for a Deputy Chief Minister role, signaling continuity as Nitish shifts to the Rajya Sabha.

title: Nitish Kumar a wily strategist who made CM seat his own
description: Nitish Kumar exits Bihar CM post after two decades, sparking political upheaval. Known for his strategic alliances and ideological shifts, Kumar carved a unique niche as an OBC leader focusing on "Mandal-plus" policies and women's welfare. His political maneuvering, including caste-based strategies and welfarism, sustained his dominance for years.

title: Amit Shah unveils ‘Pragati’, ‘Vikas’ — mascots for Census 2027
'Hasn't taken a day off in 25 years': Mark Carney praises PM Modi's work ethic
description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney lauded Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's dedication to citizen welfare. Carney highlighted Modi's focus on direct delivery of programs to ordinary Indians. He also noted Modi's relentless work ethic, stating he has not taken a day off in 25 years. Carney's remarks followed recent meetings aimed at strengthening bilateral ties.

title: 12,000 Indians in Gulf seek help to return home
Delhi Confidential: Kochi Connect
Gulf conflict: LNG squeeze may impact urea output ahead of kharif season
description: India's fertilizer supply faces potential disruptions for the upcoming kharif season if the Strait of Hormuz blockade persists. While current stocks are healthy, reduced LNG imports for urea production could impact output. Rising global prices due to shipping route blockades may also increase government subsidy expenses.

title: Sanju Samson reflects on his journey to success in the T20 World Cup
description: Samson enjoyed a slice of luck in the third over when England captain Harry Brook dropped a regulation catch at mid-on with the batter on 15— an opportunity India’s opener made England regret.

Over 60% turnout as Nepal votes in 'historic' polls powered by Gen Z
description: Nepal's Gen Z voters turned out in large numbers for snap parliamentary elections, a significant development following September protests that reshaped the country's politics. Despite logistical challenges in the mountainous nation, preliminary turnout exceeded 60%, with young activists viewing election day as the next phase of their movement for change and accountability.

'Sinners' leads this year's nominations with a record 16 nods, while performances of nominated songs 'Golden' and 'I Lied to You' are confirmed.

description: An emerging superpower should not remain a mute spectator to such violent incidents in its own region, says Mr. Gehlot; if we are the true protectors of the Indian Ocean, we must put our autonomy and safety of our guests above all else, he adds

title: Middle East tensions: PM Modi, Emmanuel Macron discuss need for dialogue & diplomacy
description: Prime Minister Modi held discussions with French President Macron, emphasizing a return to dialogue and diplomacy for the West Asia region. Both leaders committed to close engagement and coordinated efforts to restore peace and stability. Concurrently, External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar engaged with his counterparts from Saudi Arabia and Oman, underscoring India's active diplomatic outreach.

description: Iran's foreign minister has warned of severe repercussions after a US submarine torpedoed the Iranian warship IRIS Dena in international waters, resulting in 87 sailor deaths. The Indian Navy joined the search and rescue efforts following a distress call. Amidst rising tensions, another Iranian warship is seeking refuge in Sri Lankan waters.

Adah, Chandan, Chahatt, Nyrraa discuss unstable incomes and financial planning
description: Behind the glamour of red carpets, Friday releases and social media fame lies a far more complex financial reality for actors. In exclusive conversations with ETimes, actors Adah Sharma, Chahatt Khanna, Nyrraa M Banerji and Chandan K Anand spoke candidly about the economics of surviving in the entertainment industry — from financial misconceptions and savings strategies to the hidden costs of building a brand.

title: T20 World Cup: We wanted to finish off the game a bit earlier, says Samson
description: The indian wicketkeeper-batter reveals why skipper Suryakumar held back Axar in the climactic stages of the semifinal

India finally condoles killing of Iran's Khamenei; foreign ministers talk
description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri signed a condolence book at the Iranian embassy following the killing of Iran's supreme leader, amid criticism over India's initial silence. This move follows a telephonic conversation between External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and his Iranian counterpart, with a potential high-level meeting scheduled.

title: The promise of a bridge to development in Odisha’s Kalahandi
description: For tribal and Dalit families stranded on a landmass within the vast Indravati reservoir in Odisha’s Kalahandi district, life remains in limbo. Satyasundar Barik travels to this remote, rarely visited region to examine how a project widely credited with ushering in prosperity has failed to positively transform the lives of the people there

title: Nitish Kumar files nomination papers for Rajya Sabha, ending 20 years as Bihar CM
description: Confirming speculation about the end of his 20-year rule in Bihar with a post on social media, Mr. Kumar declared that he had a “desire in his heart” to become a member of both the Houses of Parliament.

title: 'Always wished to be in both Houses': Nitish Kumar files Rajya Sabha papers
description: Nitish Kumar filed for Rajya Sabha, signaling a potential end to his Bihar CM tenure despite family and party objections. His move, supported by Amit Shah, has sparked unrest within JD(U), with workers protesting. Discussions within NDA are underway for a successor, possibly a Dalit leader or JD(U) deputy CM, as RJD criticizes the political maneuvering.

title: Kalyan Singh told an ‘utter lie’: K.K. Venugopal on Babri Masjid demolition
description: In a candid conversation with The Hindu, K.K. Venugopal discusses the “command from political masters” that triggered the anti-Sikh riots in the 1980s, his sense of betrayal when he realised then-UP CM Kalyan Singh had told him an “utter lie” that the Babri Masjid would not be demolished in the 1990s.

description: Oklahoma Republican Sen. Markwayne Mullin says he has no idea how quickly his nomination to replace Kristi Noem at the Department of Homeland Security will move

title: US closes embassy in Kuwait after Iranian strikes as war spreads across Gulf
description: Amidst escalating Middle East tensions, the US Embassy in Kuwait has shut down following Iranian missile and drone strikes. These attacks, which killed six American soldiers and damaged the embassy, have spread to other Gulf capitals like Doha and Bahrain, disrupting air travel and oil shipments.

title: Rajya Sabha polls: Political tie-ups against NDA face acid test in Bihar, Odisha
description: Rajya Sabha nominations have set the stage for political contests in Bihar and Odisha, with new alignments tested. While Maharashtra's NCP factions remain divided, the BJP-led NDA is strategically nominating diverse candidates to broaden its social coalition.

title: High court fails to deliver verdict for 6 years, Supreme Court shifts case to itself
description: The Supreme Court has taken the unprecedented step of transferring three petitions from the Allahabad High Court to itself, citing a six-year delay in delivering a verdict on a 1994 criminal case. This intervention, aimed at ensuring speedy justice, highlights concerns over the High Court's prolonged inaction, which has also kept the trial stayed.

This move, occurring just before state elections, has drawn sharp criticism from Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee.  Taranjit Singh Sandhu is set to become the new L-G of Delhi, among other key appointments across various states and Union Territories.

Justices Ahsanuddin Amanullah and R Mahadevan emphasized that such practices erode public faith in the judiciary, urging strict action against disobedient parties, especially state entities, to uphold the rule of law.

description: West Asian conflict is causing airlines, especially Indian ones, significant operational challenges and fare hikes due to Pakistan airspace closure. Air India seeks approval for a new Central Asia route to bypass war zones and mitigate rising costs, including soaring insurance premiums and fuel prices. This crisis is leading to a demand-supply mismatch, drastically increasing ticket prices for passengers.

Efforts are underway to evacuate students to safer locations within Iran before flying them home.

This comes amid a US military campaign targeting Iran's capabilities, not regime change.

title: Preventing diabetes before birth, to offer hope for future generations
description: Conventional screening for gestational diabetes typically occurs in the second trimester. By then, foetal programming may already have occurred. A shift in strategy is therefore warranted: screening and intervention must occur in the first trimester — ideally by the 8th week of pregnancy

title: New York Yankees linked to $330M Philadelphia Phillies star to pair with Aaron Judge
description: The New York Yankees are reportedly exploring a trade for Bryce Harper from the Philadelphia Phillies during the 2026 preseason. Such a move would pair Harper with Aaron Judge, significantly boosting the Yankees' offensive power. This potential deal could see the Yankees send outfield depth and prospects to Philadelphia, while the Phillies would gain financial flexibility and farm system depth.

title: Connor McDavid sends clear message as Edmonton Oilers face trade deadline
description: Connor McDavid quickly endorsed the Oilers’ trade for Connor Murphy, stressing the need to add talent without subtracting key pieces. With Edmonton firmly in a win now phase and draft picks holding less value, the move reflects urgency around the Stanley Cup chase. Chicago gains a future asset, while the Oilers strengthen their blue line for an immediate playoff push.

title: Mark Zuckerberg is done with Meta's highest-paid employee, this reorganisation proves
description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has quietly dismantled Alexandr Wang's power nine months after the $14 billion hire. The 28-year-old data labeling entrepreneur now oversees AI models being built on infrastructure he doesn't control. A new applied AI engineering organization under Andrew Bosworth routes talent and data pipelines around Wang entirely. Researchers report to other executives. Wang keeps his title but loses every lever that matters—a calculated pivot that suggests Zuckerberg's bet has already failed.
